briefdescription.para: Copies n bytes from the object pointed to by s2 into the object pointed to by s1.

detaileddescription.para: If copying takes place between objects that overlap, the behavior is undefined.

briefdescription.para: Copies n bytes from the object pointed to by s2 into the object pointed to by s1.

detaileddescription.para: Copying takes place as if the n bytes from the object pointed to by s2 are first copied into a temporary array of n bytes that does not overlap the objects pointed to by s1 and s2, and then the n bytes from the temporary array are copied into the object pointed to by s1.

briefdescription.para: Compares the first n bytes (each interpreted as an unsigned char) of the object pointed to by s1 to the first n btyes of the object pointed to by s2.

detaileddescription.para: The sign of a non-zero return value shall be determined by the sign of the difference between the values of the first pair of bytes (both interpreted as type unsigned char) that differ in the objects being compared.

simplesect.para: an integer greater than, equal to, or less than 0, if the object pointed to by s1 is greater than, equal to, or less than the object pointed to by s2.

briefdescription.para: Compares the string pointed to by s1 to the string pointed to by s2.

detaileddescription.para: The sign of a non-zero return value shall be determined by the sign of the difference between the values of the first pair of bytes (both interpreted as type unsigned char) that differ in the strings being compared.

simplesect.para: an integer greater than, equal to, or less than 0, if the string pointed to by s1 is greater than, equal to, or less than the string pointed to by s2.

briefdescription.para: Compares not more than n bytes (bytes that follow a null character are not compared) from the array pointed to by s1 to the array pointed to by s2.

detaileddescription.para: The sign of a non-zero return value shall be determined by the sign of the difference between the values of the first pair of bytes (both interpreted as type unsigned char) that differ in the strings being compared.

simplesect.para: an integer greater than, equal to, or less than 0, if the possible null-terminated array pointed to by s1 is greater than, equal to, or less than the possibly null-terminated array pointed to by s2.

briefdescription.para: Copies c (converted to an unsigned char) into each of the first n bytes of the object pointed to by s.
Computes the length of the string pointed to by s.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes that precede the terminating null character.

briefdescription.para: Computes the length of the string at s, not including the terminating null byte.

detaileddescription.para: At most maxlen characters are examined.

simplesect.para: the smaller of the length of the string at s or maxlen.

briefdescription.para: This header file is part of the C11 and POSIX compatibility library; it includes <string.h> and defines any missing functionality.

detaileddescription.para:
